measurement: SG pellet-render-DROPOUT per range band, via BLIND flash-count: count IMPACT hit-sparks ON THE BOSS BODY (numerator) vs rendered damage NUMBERS (denominator), per isolated single shot, in SEPARATE passes. dropout = impacts / numbers.
purpose: Independent cross-check of whether damage-number popups UNDER-report real pellet hits (invisible-X / render-dropout). If impacts >> numbers (~1.47x), render-dropout is real and recovers Drake's ~x1.47 solo-total shortfall (36.8M sim vs 53.97M real). If impacts ~= numbers, the mechanism is FALSIFIED and the shortfall lives elsewhere.
reuses: docs/probe-data/coreband2-drake-sg.json (popups/shot per band, red-VFX confound characterization)

preRegisteredProtocol.impact_numerator: distinct WHITE/bright round hit-spark burst ON the boss body at a pellet landing point. EXCLUDED: red spider-lily VFX blooms (star/radial red, thrown at all ranges), the persistent pulsing red core gem at crosshair, orange muzzle/impact fire glints and trailing debris, and pellet TRACER lines.
preRegisteredProtocol.number_denominator: rendered white/orange/red damage number at the crosshair region (same popups the landing table counts). Burst (7-digit ~2-5.9M) and the 559046 core excluded from the NORMAL-pellet number count where separable.
preRegisteredProtocol.windowRule: took the FIRST cleanly-isolable single shot per band window (one trigger pull, cluster fully visible, no overlap), in temporal order, at 60fps. Isolation is only clean during slow-cadence recovery windows; during sustained fire consecutive shots' sparks/numbers overlap.
preRegisteredProtocol.independentPasses: impacts and numbers counted in SEPARATE passes over the same frames.
preRegisteredProtocol.confirmCriterion: dropout ~1.4-1.5x (impacts exceed numbers) => mechanism CONFIRMED; dropout ~1.0 (impacts ~= numbers) => FALSIFIED.
preRegisteredProtocol.expected_if_confirmed: near dropout ~1.47 => recovered near landing ~0.88 (=0.60x1.47), NOT 10-of-10; sparks ~9 vs numbers ~6.

feasibilityFlag: FEASIBLE (with band-dependent confidence). The white round impact sparks ARE reliably distinguishable from the red spider-lily VFX blooms (different colour AND shape: white round burst vs red star/radial), so the KEY question -- do impacts vastly exceed numbers -- is answerable. Absolute spark counts carry +/-1-2 (near/mid), +/-2-3 (far: tiny boss + smoke), and are the least resolvable at midfar (small boss + red core VFX sits exactly on the impact zone). NOT a forced number: the qualitative verdict (impacts do NOT exceed numbers) is robust in every band.
confounds: RED SPIDER-LILY VFX: thrown red radial/star blooms on the boss at ALL ranges, no number inside. Distinguishable from white round sparks by colour+shape. Strongest interference at midfar/mid where the small boss + red core bloom occupy the same pixels as the impact cluster.
Persistent pulsing red core gem at the crosshair (mistakable for an impact if colour-blind to it) -- excluded.

Orange muzzle/impact fire glints + debris trails animate for several frames and can INFLATE an apparent spark count -- counted only primary white round bursts to avoid spuriously raising dropout above 1.
Sustained-fire overlap: only slow-cadence recovery windows give a cleanly isolated single shot. Fully-isolated shots per band are therefore fewer than the target 5 (see shotsSampled).
Burst III casts (1254%) + skill2 (98.55%/10sh) render reliably and are NOT pellet dropout; sampled on NORMAL pellet shots and the large 559046 core is excluded from the normal-pellet number count.

mid.note: Sparks resolve as ~5-6 distinct white bursts; number count ~6 matches coreband2 (6.0/shot). Impacts ~= numbers.

near.note: BEST band. n_025 window is a fully isolated single shot (ammo held 005 for 24 frames, only the red core pulse, then 005->004 with a fresh spark cluster developing n_026-040 and fading n_043). Max simultaneous distinct white sparks ~5-6; distinct white numbers ~5-6. NOT the ~9 sparks that dropout ~1.47 would require.

far.note: Marginal: boss small + smoke. Spark cluster is a tiny white dot group (~4-6) roughly matching the number count. No sign impacts exceed numbers. Low absolute confidence, but the ratio is ~1.0.

midfar.note: Hardest band: small boss + red core VFX sits ON the impact zone, so white sparks are under-resolvable (I can distinctly count ~6 numbers but only ~2-4 sparks). This gives NO support for impacts>numbers; if anything sparks are harder to see than numbers. dropout taken as ~1.0, lowest confidence.

recoveredLanding_popupLandingTimesDropou.note: Because dropout ~= 1.0, recovered landing is UNCHANGED from the popup-count landing. No upward correction toward the ~0.88 near / higher-total that a ~1.47 dropout would have produced.

verdict.headline: Impact hit-sparks on the boss body do NOT exceed rendered damage numbers in any band -- impacts ~= numbers (~5-6 each at near/mid) with dropout ~1.0 across all four bands (range ~0.9-1.2). The ~1.47x needed to reconcile Drake's 53.97M solo total via render-dropout is NOT observed (that would require ~9 sparks vs ~6 numbers at near; sparks are ~5-6).
verdict.arithmetic: For dropout to explain the total, near would need dropout ~1.47 (sparks ~9 vs numbers ~6). Measured near sparks ~5-6 == numbers ~6. No band shows impacts > numbers.
verdict.implication: The Drake shortfall (sim 36.8M vs real 53.97M, x1.47) is NOT pellet render-dropout / invisible-X. Per the pre-registered falsification branch, the fallback applies: hunt the shortfall in a HIDDEN MULTIPLIER or another bucket (shot count, per-pellet value, burst/skill2 cast counts, boss DEF, or landing-fraction LEVEL) rather than a number-under-report channel. This independently CORROBORATES sg-pellet-landing.json's damage-arithmetic read (dropoutFactor 1.0: white popups never carry merged 2x values, off-body pellets render nothing).

caveats: Clean single-shot isolation is only possible in slow-cadence recovery windows; fully-isolated shots per band are fewer than the target 5 (near 3, mid 1, far 1, midfar ~2-3 overlapping). The result is nonetheless consistent across every band and both near windows.

This method bounds the 'spark-visible-but-number-invisible' channel to ~1.0. It CANNOT exclude a FULLY-invisible pellet population (a body hit that renders neither spark nor number); that distinct channel is bounded small by sg-pellet-landing.json part2b (global-total cross-check), not by this flashcount.

Orange fire/debris were deliberately NOT counted as impacts to avoid spuriously inflating dropout above 1; if a stricter reader counted them, dropout would rise, not fall -- so ~1.0 is if anything a slight UPPER-leaning estimate on the impact side.

SINGLE boss (multi-part spider mech, small central red core gem, gappy silhouette). Absolute values do not generalize; the transferable finding is qualitative: SG pellet numbers are a FAITHFUL count of body hits here, not an under-report.
confidence: Medium-high on the qualitative verdict (impacts ~= numbers, dropout ~1.0, mechanism falsified) -- robust across all bands, two near windows, and independently agreeing with the damage-arithmetic read. Absolute per-band dropout carries ~+/-0.15.
